title: Cursor details Auto-review for governing coding-agent autonomy
friendlyTitle: Cursor adds smarter safety checks for coding agents
summary: Cursor described Auto-review, a default feature for new users that uses a classifier agent to review proposed actions before execution. The system treats autonomy as a dial: low-risk actions proceed with fewer interruptions, while high-consequence actions can be blocked or routed to safer alternatives.
friendlySummary: Cursor explained Auto-review, a feature that checks what a coding agent is about to do and decides whether it is safe in context. It aims to reduce constant approval pop-ups while still slowing agents down when risk is higher.
whyItMatters: As coding agents gain file, terminal, and MCP access, approval prompts alone do not scale. Context-aware review is a practical pattern for making agents useful without training users to click ‘allow’ on everything.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This is useful because people ignore too many warnings. Smarter checks can let agents move quickly on safe work and pause when files, credentials, or production systems are involved.
